title: 5 signs you're not running your own life
caption:
If something else is running your life, you can feel it.

You hit snooze until your body drags you out of bed. You pick up the phone before you've said a word to God. You say yes to things you knew you should say no to, and you tell yourself you'll deal with it later. You've been "working on" the same sin for two years. And the people closest to you — she, your mom, your roommate — they absorb what you haven't handled.

Proverbs 25:28 says a man with no self-control is like a city with broken-down walls. Anybody walks in.

The governed man isn't perfect. He's just stopped negotiating with his own weakness. He made a decision about who's in charge, and he keeps making it — at 6 AM, in the quiet before the screen, in the moment before he snaps, in the conversation with his brother.

Self-mastery isn't a personality type. It's a daily vote. You get to cast it again tomorrow.

—

Comment "govern" and I'll send you the link to ETHOS.

theme: He runs his life — his impulses don't control him.
